meta.title: Open Banking Regulations Global Dataset
meta.description: Structured dataset of open banking regulatory frameworks by country and region, covering API standards, compliance requirements, and implementation status.
meta.disclaimer: Regulations evolve rapidly. Verify current requirements with local legal counsel before implementation.

status_definitions.live: Regulation is in force with live API ecosystem
status_definitions.phased: Regulation partially live; full rollout in progress

regulations.name: UK Open Banking
regulations.regulatory_body: Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) / Payment Systems Regulator (PSR)
legislation: CMA Retail Banking Market Investigation Order 2017
regulations.tpp_registration: FCA authorisation as AISP or PISP required
regulations.api_rate_limits: Defined per implementation; CMA9 must publish
regulations.data_coverage: Transactions 24 months; standing orders; direct debits; balances

regulations.name: PSD2 / Open Finance
legislation: EBA RTS on SCA and CSC
regulations.api_standard: No single mandated standard; Berlin Group NextGenPSD2 most common
regulations.covered_institutions: All payment account providers in EU/EEA
regulations.tpp_registration: NCA (National Competent Authority) authorisation per member state
regulations.upcoming: PSD3 and Payment Services Regulation (PSR) proposed 2024; expanded to non-payment accounts (open finance)

regulations.name: FIDA (Financial Data Access)
regulations.scope: Extends open banking to insurance, investments, pensions, mortgages, crypto

regulations.name: CFPB Rule 1033 (Open Banking)
legislation: CFPB Personal Financial Data Rights Rule (October 2024)

phase_timeline.phase_1: April 2026: largest banks (assets > $500B) and largest data aggregators

regulations.api_standard: No mandated standard; FDX API de facto industry standard
regulations.screen_scraping: Covered institutions must offer API; screen scraping may be restricted
regulations.tpp_registration: FDX membership encouraged; no federal licensing yet
regulations.covered_institutions: Depository institutions, credit card issuers, digital wallets

regulations.name: Consumer Data Right (CDR)
regulations.regulatory_body: Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) / OAIC
regulations.api_standard: DSB (Data Standards Body) Consumer Data Standards
regulations.covered_institutions: Major banks (2020); mid-tier banks; all ADIs by 2023
regulations.adr_registration: ACCC accreditation as Accredited Data Recipient (ADR) required

regulations.name: Open Finance Brasil
regulations.covered_institutions: Mandatory for authorized financial institutions with 10M+ active customers

regulations.name: Account Aggregator Framework
regulations.regulatory_body: Reserve Bank of India (RBI) / SEBI / IRDAI
regulations.model: Consent-based data sharing via Account Aggregator NBFC intermediaries
regulations.participant_count: AA ecosystem: 5 live AAs; 100+ FIPs; 50+ FIUs

regulations.name: SGFinDex / APIX
legislation: No specific mandate; voluntary industry initiative led by MAS
regulations.covered_institutions: Major banks voluntary participants; government portal (MyInfo)
products: Financial data aggregation for personal finance

regulations.name: Consumer-Driven Banking
regulations.regulatory_body: Financial Consumer Agency of Canada (FCAC) / Department of Finance
regulations.api_standard: To be determined; FDX API likely
regulations.current_state: Screen scraping common; Flinks, Plaid, Finicity operational without legal mandate

regulations.name: Ley Fintech (Open Finance)
legislation: Ley para Regular las Instituciones de Tecnologia Financiera (2018)
regulations.adoption_rate: Slow due to secondary regulation delays

regulations.name: CBN Open Banking Framework
legislation: CBN Regulation on Open Banking in Nigeria 2023

tiers.tier_1: Account information and transaction data sharing

regulations.covered_institutions: Banks, mobile money operators, payment service providers

regulations.name: MyData Service
legislation: Credit Information Use and Protection Act (amended 2020)

regulations.name: Open API Framework
regulations.covered_institutions: Retail banks in HK; voluntary framework

regulations.name: Open Banking Policy
regulations.regulatory_body: Financial Services Agency (FSA) / Ministry of Finance
regulations.covered_institutions: Major banks mandated; regional banks encouraged
regulations.api_standard: FISC (Center for Financial Industry Information Systems) guidelines
regulations.tpp_registration: Electronic Payment Services Provider (EPSP) registration with FSA
regulations.adoption_rate: Moderate; major banks live; some regional variation

regulations.name: UAE Open Finance
regulations.regulatory_body: Central Bank of UAE (CBUAE) / DIFC / ADGM
regulations.api_standard: CBUAE Open Finance Technical Standards (under development)

regulations.name: Consumer Data Right (NZ)
regulations.regulatory_body: Ministry of Business, Innovation and Employment (MBIE) / MBIE/RBNZ
legislation: Customer and Product Data Act 2025 (awaiting royal assent)

regulations.name: Saudi Open Banking
regulations.covered_institutions: SAMA-licensed banks; fintech licensed by SAMA

regulations.name: Kenya Open Banking
legislation: CBK Draft Open Banking Policy Framework 2023
regulations.current_state: M-Pesa API de facto open banking; formal framework in consultation
regulations.mpesa_api: Safaricom M-Pesa API widely used for payment integration without formal OB mandate

regulations.name: Thailand Open Banking
legislation: BOT Payment Systems Roadmap; Prompt Pay ecosystem

regulations.name: South Africa Open Banking
regulations.regulatory_body: South African Reserve Bank (SARB) / National Treasury
legislation: SARB Policy Paper on Open Finance 2024
regulations.current_state: Industry working groups; no formal mandate yet; Stitch, Peach Payments enabling screen-scraping based aggregation

regulations.name: EU Instant Payments Regulation
requirements: All EU PSPs must offer euro instant credit transfers by October 2025
Price parity with standard credit transfers
OFAC/sanctions screening within 10 seconds of transaction
regulations.covers: Eurozone banks; non-euro EU banks have extended timeline

regulations.name: Bahrain Open Banking Framework
regulations.note: First open banking framework in the Middle East
